title: duma horrida
intro: Duma horrida, commonly known as Devil's Claw, is a plant species native to the southern parts of Africa. The health benefits of Duma horrida include its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and swelling, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. The therapeutic actions of Duma horrida are primarily due to its bioactive constituents, including harpagoside and phlegmaroside, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. Additionally, Duma horrida has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including arthritis, fever, and digestive issues.
intro_study: According to "Inflammopharmacology", Duma horrida (also known as Asparagus dumosus) benefits include reducing arthritic indices, chronic inflammatory and oxidative stress biomarkers, and down-regulating mRNA expression of arthritic candidate genes, thus exhibiting anti-arthritic effects.
side_effects: Duma horrida can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, nausea, and vomiting due to its toxic alkaloids. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can also cause dermatitis, rashes, and allergic reactions in some individuals. In severe cases, improper handling of the duma horrida plant can lead to respiratory issues, including bronchitis and asthma-like symptoms. Furthermore, ingesting even a small amount of the plant's leaves or roots can cause cardiac problems, including arrhythmias and decreased blood pressure.
